name: indicator
description: Positions an overlay element (badge, dot, count) at a corner of its child

meta.name: Indicator
meta.description: Positions an overlay element (badge, dot, count) at a corner of its child

props.name: overlay
props.description: The element to display at the corner (badge, dot, count)

examples.title: Notification count
examples.description: Notification count badge on an icon button

examples.title: Online status
examples.description: Online status dot on an avatar

examples.title: Bottom-start placement
examples.description: Indicator positioned at the bottom-start corner

whenNotToUse: Inline badges within text — use Badge directly
Status messages below a field — use a form hint or Alert

related.name: Badge
related.reason: Badge is the most common overlay content placed inside Indicator

related.name: Avatar
related.reason: Indicator is frequently used to attach a status dot to an Avatar

intent.a11yRationale: The overlay is marked aria-hidden because it is a visual affordance — the underlying control (button, avatar) carries its own accessible label. Screen-reader users should receive the count or status through the control's accessible name or a live region, not from the overlay div.

flexibility.note: All four corners are supported; top-end is the most common convention for notification counts
